title: cynoglossum coeruleum
intro: Cynoglossum coeruleum, also known as the forget-me-not of the Himalayas, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels. The health benefits of Cynoglossum coeruleum include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve cognitive function, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Cynoglossum coeruleum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it has also been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to make a powdered extract for medicinal purposes.
